name: LaVague
tagline: Build AI web agents that turn a goal into real browser actions
seoTitle: LaVague: Open-Source AI Web Agent Framework
metaDescription: LaVague is an open-source Python framework for building AI web agents that take a plain objective and run it as Selenium or Playwright browser actions.
overview: LaVague is an open-source Python framework for building AI web agents that automate tasks in a browser. You give an agent an objective in plain language, such as "Go on the quicktour of PEFT," and it works out the steps needed and runs them on a real web page.

Each agent is made of two parts: a World Model that reads the current page and the objective and produces instructions, and an Action Engine that compiles those instructions into browser code and executes it over Selenium or Playwright. There is also a Chrome extension driver.

It is aimed at developers who want to automate web processes for their end users. Within the agent-frameworks space, LaVague sits in the computer- and browser-use niche, focusing on driving an actual browser rather than calling APIs. A related tool, LaVague QA, builds on the framework to turn Gherkin specs into web tests.
features: World Model that converts an objective plus the current page state into a set of instructions
Action Engine that compiles instructions into Selenium or Playwright code and runs them
Three driver options: Selenium WebDriver, Playwright, and a Chrome extension driver
Built-in contexts and customizable configuration for swapping models and settings
Token counter for estimating token usage and costs, plus logging and debugging tools
Optional interactive Gradio interface for trying agents from a UI

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package, set your OpenAI API key, then build a web agent with a driver, a World Model, and an Action Engine. The default configuration uses OpenAI, so set OPENAI_API_KEY in your environment first.

steps.heading: Install LaVague
steps.body: Install the framework from PyPI with pip.

steps.heading: Set your OpenAI API key
steps.body: The default configuration uses the OpenAI API, so export a valid key in your local environment.

steps.heading: Build and run a web agent
steps.body: Create a Selenium driver, a World Model, and an Action Engine, then give the agent a page and an objective in plain language.
steps.code:
from lavague.core import WorldModel, ActionEngine
from lavague.core.agents import WebAgent
from lavague.drivers.selenium import SeleniumDriver

selenium_driver = SeleniumDriver(headless=False)
world_model = WorldModel()
action_engine = ActionEngine(selenium_driver)
agent = WebAgent(world_model, action_engine)
agent.get("https://huggingface.co/docs")
agent.run("Go on the quicktour of PEFT")

# Launch Gradio Agent Demo
agent.demo("Go on the quicktour of PEFT")

useCases: Automate repetitive web processes for end users by describing the goal instead of scripting each click
Navigate documentation or product pages to locate and follow a specific flow, such as a quicktour
Generate web tests from Gherkin specs using the LaVague QA tooling built on the framework
Prototype browser-use agents while estimating token cost with the built-in token counter
